1.1: The vision of Isaiah son of Amoz that he saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem in the days of Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah, kings of Judah.
1.2: Hear, O heavens, and give ear, O earth, for YHWH has spoken: “Sons I have brought up and raised up, and they have rebelled against Me.
1.3: An ox has known his owner and a donkey the trough of its master; Israel has not known; my people have not understood.”
1.4: Alas, O sinning nation, a people heavy with iniquity, a seed doing evil, sons bringing corruption! They have forsaken YHWH, they have despised the Holy One of Israel, they have become estranged backward.
1.5: For what should you? be struck still? Should you? continue in rebellion? The whole head is sick, and the whole heart is faint.
1.6: From the sole of the foot and to the head, no soundness is in it—wounds and fresh stripes and blows; they have not been pressed and not bound up and not soothed with oil.
1.7: Your? land is a desolation, your? cities burnt with fire, your? ground in front of you?, strangers devour it, and it is a desolation, as overthrown by strangers.
1.8: And the daughter of Zion has been left like a shelter in a vineyard, like a lodge in a cucumber field, like a city besieged.
1.9: Unless YHWH of Hosts had left to us a very small remnant, we would have become like Sodom, we would have resembled Gomorrah.
1.10: Hear the word of YHWH, chiefs of Sodom; give ear to the Instruction of our God, people of Gomorrah!
1.11: “What to Me is the multitude of your? sacrifices?” Says YHWH. “I have had my fill of burnt offerings of rams, and the fat of well-fed cattle, and in the blood of bulls and lambs and he-goats I take no delight.
1.12: When you? come to appear before My face, who has sought this from your? hand, to trample My courts?
1.13: You? shall not add to bring an offering of worthlessness; incense, it is an abomination to Me, New Moons and Sabbaths and the calling of assemblies—I cannot endure iniquity, in a solemn assembly!
1.14: Your? New Moons and your? appointed feasts, My soul hates; they are to Me as a burden; I am weary of bearing them .
1.15: And in your? spreading forth your? hands, I will hide My eyes from you?; even though you? multiply prayers, I will not be listening. Your? hands are full of blood!
1.16: Wash, cleanse yourselves; turn aside the evil of your? deeds from before My eyes. Cease to do evil.
1.17: Learn to do good, seek justice, rebuke the oppressor, bring justice for the fatherless, contend for the widow.”
1.18: “Come now, and let us reason together,” says YHWH. “Though your? sins may be like scarlet, they will be white like snow; though they are red like crimson, they will become like wool.
1.19: If you? are willing and obey, then the good of the land you? will eat.
1.20: And if you? refuse and rebel, by the sword you? will be devoured.” For the mouth of YHWH has spoken.
1.21: How the faithful city has become like a prostitute! She was full of justice, righteousness lodged with her—and now murderers are there !
1.22: Your silver has become as dross, your wine is weakened with water.
1.23: Your princes are rebels and companions of thieves, everyone is loving a bribe and pursuing rewards. The fatherless they do not defend and the case of the widow does not come to them.
1.24: Thus is the declaration of the Lord YHWH of hosts, the Mighty One of Israel: “Ah, I will be relieved of My adversaries, and avenge Myself on My enemies.
1.25: And I will turn My hand against you and will refine as with lye your dross, and I will turn aside all your alloys.
1.26: And I will restore your judges as at the first, and your counselors as at the beginning. After this it shall be called for you the city of righteousness—the Faithful City.”
1.27: Zion with justice shall be redeemed, and her repentant ones with righteousness.
1.28: And the breaking of rebels and sinners will be together, and those abandoning YHWH will be consumed.
1.29: For they will be ashamed of the Oaks that you? have desired, and you? will be embarrassed by the gardens that you? have chosen.
1.30: For you? will be like an oak at the withering of its leaf, and a garden for which there is no water.
1.31: And the strong one will become as tinder, and his work as a spark, and both of them will burn together, and none will quench them !

2.1: The word that Isaiah son of Amoz saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem:
2.2: And it shall come to pass at the end of the days, the mountain of the house of YHWH will be established at the top of the mountains, and it will be lifted up above the hills, and all the nations will flow to it.
2.3: And many people will come and say, “Come and let us go up to the mountain of YHWH, to the house of the God of Jacob; and He will teach us His ways, and we will walk in His paths.” For the law will go forth from Zion, and the Word of YHWH from Jerusalem.
2.4: And He will judge between the nations and will give rebuke to many peoples, and they will beat their swords into plowshares, and their spears into pruning hooks. Nation will no longer lift up a sword against nation, and will not they learn any more war.
2.5: O House of Jacob, come, and let us walk in the light of YHWH.
2.6: For You have left Your people, the house of Jacob, because they are filled from the east, and they practice divination like the Philistines, and with the sons of foreigners they strike hands.
2.7: And its land is full of silver and gold, and there is no end to the treasure. And its land is full of horses, and there is no end to the chariots.
2.8: And its land is full of idols; to the work of the hands they bow down—to that which its fingers have made.
2.9: And mankind is bent down, each man abases himself—do not forgive them!
2.10: Enter into the rock and hide yourself in the dust, from the dread of YHWH and from the glory of His majesty.
2.11: The haughtiness of the eyes of man will be brought low, and the loftiness of men will be bent down. And YHWH alone will be exalted in that day.
2.12: For the day of YHWH of hosts is upon everything proud and lofty, and upon everything lifted up—and it will be brought low—
2.13: and upon all the cedars of Lebanon, those being high and lifted up, and upon all the Oaks of Bashan,
2.14: and upon all on the mountains that are high, and upon all on the hills that are lifted up,
2.15: and upon every high tower, and upon every fortified wall,
2.16: and upon all the ships of Tarshish, and upon all the desirable vessels.
2.17: And the haughtiness of man will be bent down, and the loftiness of men will be brought low, and YHWH alone will be exalted in that day—
2.18: and the idols utterly will pass away.
2.19: And they will go into the caves of rocks, and into the holes of the earth, from the face of the dread of YHWH and the glory of His majesty, in His rising to shake the earth.
2.20: In that day man will throw away the idols of his silver, and idols of his gold, which they made, each for himself, to bow down to diggers of holes and to the bats,
2.21: to go into the crevices of the rocks, and into the clefts of the cliffs, before the face of the dread of YHWH and the glory of His majesty, in His rising to shake the earth.
2.22: Cease yourselves from man, whose breath is in his nostril, for of what account is he?

3.1: For behold, the Lord YHWH of hosts is turning away from Jerusalem and from Judah the staff and support—the whole staff of bread, and the whole staff of water—
3.2: the mighty one and the man of war, the judge and the prophet, and the diviner and the elder,
3.3: the captain of fifty, and the lifted up of faces, and the counselor, and the cunning magician, and the discerning charmer.
3.4: “And I will give youths as their princes, and infants will rule over them.”
3.5: And the people will be oppressed, each man by a man and each man by his neighbor; the child will rage against the elder, and the one despised against the honored.
3.6: For a man will take hold on his brother in the house of his father: “A cloak is unto you—the chief you will be for us, and this heap of ruins is under your hand;”
3.7: he will lift up in that day to say, “I will not be one binding wounds , and in my house there is no bread and no garment; you? shall not set me as chief of the people.”
3.8: For Jerusalem has stumbled, and Judah has fallen, because their tongue and their deeds are against YHWH, defying the eyes of His glory.
3.9: The look of their faces answers against them, and their sin like Sodom they declare—they do not hide it ! Woe to their soul, for they have brought evil upon themselves.
3.10: Say to the righteous that it is good, for the fruit of their deeds they will eat.
3.11: Woe to the wicked—evil; for the recompense of his hands will be done to him!
3.12: My people—their oppressors are foolish children, and women rule over them. O My people, those who guide you lead you astray, and the way of your paths they have swallowed up.
3.13: YHWH Is taking His stand to contend, and is standing to judge the peoples.
3.14: YHWH will enter into judgment with the elders of His people and His princes: “And you?—you? have consumed the vineyard; the plunder of the poor is in your? houses.
3.15: Why do you crush My people, and grind the faces of the poor?” A declaration of the Lord YHWH of hosts.
3.16: And says YHWH: “Because that the daughters of Zion are haughty, and they walk with outstretched neck and wanton eyes, walking and skipping as they go, and with their feet jingling,
3.17: and the Lord will bring scabs to the crown of the head of the daughters of Zion, and YHWH will make their foreheads bare.
3.18: That in the day the Lord will turn away the beauty of the anklets and the headbands, and the crescents,
3.19: the pendants, and the bracelets, and the veils,
3.20: the headdresses, and the armlets, and the sashes, and the boxes of perfume, and the charms,
3.21: the signet rings, and nose rings,
3.22: the festive robes, and the tunics, and the cloaks, and the purses,
3.23: and the mirrors, and the linen garments , and the turbans, and the veils.
3.24: And it will come to pass, instead of of spice, that there will be rottenness; and instead of a belt, a rope; and instead of worked hair, baldness; and instead of a fine robe, a girding of sackcloth; and shame instead of beauty.
3.25: Your men will fall by the sword, and your mighty ones in battle;
3.26: and her gates will lament and mourn and she will be left empty—on the ground she will sit.

4.1: Seven women will take hold of one of man in that day, saying, “Our own bread we will eat and our own garment we will wear; only let your name be called over us. Remove our reproach.”
4.2: That in the day will be the Branch of YHWH for beauty and for honor, and the fruit of the earth for pride and for glory to the escaped of Israel.
4.3: And it will come to pass that the remainder in Zion, and the leftover in Jerusalem, holy will be called to him—everyone who is written for life in Jerusalem—
4.4: when the Lord has washed away the filth of the daughters of Zion, and the blood of Jerusalem He has cleansed from within her, by the spirit of judgment and by the spirit of burning.
4.5: And YHWH will create over all the foundation of Mount Zion, and over her assemblies, a cloud by day, and smoke, and the shining of a flaming fire by night; for over all of the glory will be a canopy.
4.6: And a covered shelter there will be for shade by day from the heat, and for a place of refuge, and for a hiding place, from the storm and from the rain.

5.1: Let me sing now for my beloved, a song of my beloved regarding his vineyard: Unto my beloved there was a vineyard, a son of fatness on a hill.
5.2: And he dug it and cleared it of stones, and he planted it with the choicest vine; and he built a tower in its midst, and also a winepress he hewed out in it. And he waited for it to make grapes, and it made wild grapes.
5.3: “And now, O dweller of Jerusalem and man of Judah, judge please, between Me and between My vineyard.
5.4: What could have been done more for My vineyard, that I have not done in it? Why—I waited for it to make grapes—and it made wild grapes?
5.5: And now I will let you? know what I am going to do to My vineyard: I will take away its hedge, and it will be consumed and the breaking down of its wall, and it will be free for trampling.
5.6: And I will set it as a waste—it will not be pruned nor hoed, and there will come up brier and thorn; and also the clouds I will command against raining on it with rain.”
5.7: For the vineyard of YHWH of Hosts is the house of Israel, and the men of Judah are the plant of His delight; and He looked for justice, and behold, bloodshed—for righteousness, and behold, outcry.
5.8: Woe to those joining house to house; field to field they bring near, until there is no place to stand, and you? are made to dwell by yourselves alone in the midst of the land!
5.9: In my hearing, YHWH of Hosts says : “Surely many houses will be a desolation—great and beautiful ones without one dwelling!
5.10: For a ten yoke vineyard will yield one bath, and a homer of seed will yield an ephah.
5.11: Woe to those rising early in the morning to pursue strong drink, tarrying into the twilight as wine inflames them!
5.12: Are the lyre and harp and tambourine, and flute and wine, are at their feasts; and the work of YHWH they do not regard, and the work of His hands they do not see.
5.13: Therefore my people have gone into captivity from lack of knowledge; and its honored men go hungry, and its multitude is parched with thirst.
5.14: Therefore Sheol has enlarged her soul and has opened her mouth beyond measure; her nobles will go down—and her crowd and her revelers and exulting—into her.
5.15: And mankind is bent down, and each man is brought low, and the eyes of the haughty will be brought down.
5.16: YHWH of Hosts will be exalted in justice, and the Holy God will show Himself holy in righteousness.
5.17: And the lambs will graze as in their pasture, and in the waste places of the fat ones, sojourners will eat.
5.18: Woe to those drawing out iniquity with cords of falsehood, and sin with a rope of the cart,
5.19: saying, “Let Him hurry, let Him hurry His work, that we may see it ! Let the counsel of the Holy One of Israel draw near and come, that we may know it !”
5.20: Woe to those saying to evil “Good,” and to good “Evil,” putting darkness for light and light for darkness, putting bitter for sweet and sweet for bitter!
5.21: Woe to the wise ones in their own eyes, and before their own faces discerning.
5.22: Woe to the mighty at drinking wine and men of strength in mixing strong drink,
5.23: those declaring right the wicked for a bribe, and justice for the righteous ones they turn aside from him.
5.24: Therefore as the tongue of fire devours the chaff and the flame consumes the stubble, their root will be as rottenness, and their blossom will go up like dust, because they have rejected the instruction of YHWH of hosts, and the Word of the Holy One of Israel they have despised.
5.25: Thus the anger of YHWH burns against His people; and therefore He has stretched out His hand against them and struck them, and the hills trembled; and their carcasses became like refuse in the midst of the streets. In all this, His anger is not turned away, and still His hand is stretched out.
5.26: And He will lift up a signal for the nations from afar, and He will whistle to them from the end of the earth—and behold, swiftly with haste it will come!
5.27: No one will be weary nor stumbling among them; he will not will he slumber and not sleep, and not will be loosed the girdle of his loins, and not will be broken the strap of his sandals—
5.28: whose arrows are sharp, and all his bows bent; the hooves of his horses like flint are reckoned, and his wheels like a whirlwind.
5.29: The roaring of him is like a lion—he roars like the young lions, and growls and seizes prey and carries it away safely, and there is no one delivering.
5.30: And he will roar over it that on the day, like the roaring of the sea. And one looks over the land, and behold, darkness and distress, and the light is darkened by its clouds.

6.1: And in the year of the death of King Uzziah, I saw the Lord sitting on a throne being high and lifted up, and his train was filling the temple.
6.2: Seraphim stood above Him, with six wings for each—six wings—with two he covered his face, and with two he covered his feet, and with two he flew.
6.3: And this one cried to this one and said: “Holy, holy, holy is YHWH of Hosts; the fullness of all the earth is His glory!”
6.4: And the posts of the doors were shaken from the voice of the one calling, and the house was filled with smoke.
6.5: And I said, “Woe to me, for I am finished! For I am a man of unclean lips, and I am dwelling in the midst of a people of unclean lips; for the King YHWH of Hosts, my eyes have seen!”
6.6: And flew to me one from the seraphim, and in his hand was a hot coal he had taken from the altar with tongs.
6.7: And he touched it upon my mouth and said, “Behold, this has touched upon your lips; and is turned aside your iniquity, and your sin is wiped away.”
6.8: And I heard the voice of the Lord, saying, “Whom will I send, and who will go for Us?” And I said “Here am I—send me!”
6.9: And He said, “Go and say to the people this: ‘Be ever hearing and never understanding, and be ever seeing and never knowing.’
6.10: Fatten the heart of this people, and its ears make heavy and its eyes shut, lest it see with its eyes, hear and with its ears, and understand with its heart, and turn back and there be healing for it.
6.11: And I said, “Until when, O Lord?” And He answered, “Until when the cities lie in ruins without one dwelling, and the houses without man, and the land lies in ruins as a desolation,
6.12: and YHWH has removed man far away, and are many the forsaken places in the midst of the land.
6.13: And still are in it a tenth, and will it return and be for burning, like the terebinth and oak that in falling is a stump in them. The seed of holiness is its stump.”

7.1: And it came to pass in the days of Ahaz son of Jotham, son of Uzziah, king of Judah, that came up Rezin king of Aram, and Pekah son of Remaliah, king of Israel, to Jerusalem to war against it. He was not able to fight against it.
7.2: And it was declared to the house of David, saying, Aram has rested itself upon Ephraim. His heart and the heart of his people was shaken, as are shaken the trees of the forest from the face of the wind.
7.3: And YHWH said to Isaiah, “Go out now to meet Ahaz, you and Shear-jashub your son, toward the end of the conduit of the upper pool, toward the road of the Field of the Washer.
7.4: And say to him: Take heed and be quiet. Do not be afraid and do not be soft because of these two stubs of smoldering firebrands, with the burning of the anger of Rezin and Aram and the son of Remaliah,
7.5: because indeed Aram has plotted evil against you, with Ephraim and the son of Remaliah, saying,
7.6: ‘Let us go up against Judah and treat it with contempt and split it apart unto ourselves, and in its midst we will make the son of Tabeal reign as king.’
7.7: Thus says the Lord YHWH: ‘It will not stand, and it will not come to pass.
7.8: For the head of Aram is Damascus, and the head of Damascus is Rezin, and within sixty-five years Ephraim will be shattered as a people.
7.9: And the head of Ephraim is Samaria, and the head of Samaria is the son of Remaliah. If you? do not stand firm in faith, surely you? will not stand firm.’”
7.10: And YHWH continued to speak to Ahaz, saying,
7.11: “Ask for yourself a sign from YHWH your God; make the request deep, or make it high above.”
7.12: And Ahaz said, “I will not ask, and I will not test YHWH.”
7.13: And he said, “Hear now, O house of David: Is it a small thing for you? to weary men, that you? would also weary my God?
7.14: Therefore the Lord Himself will give to you? a sign: Behold the virgin is conceiving and is bringing forth a son, and she will call His name Immanuel.
7.15: Curds and honey he will eat, when he knows to refuse the evil and to choose the good.
7.16: For before the youth knows to reject the evil and to choose the good, the land that you dread will be forsaken because of the two of her kings.
7.17: YHWH will bring upon you and upon your people and upon the house of your father days that have not come since the day of the turning away of Ephraim from Judah—the king of Assyria!”
7.18: And it will come to pass in that day, that YHWH will whistle for the fly that is at the farthest of the rivers of Egypt, and for the bee that is in the land of Assyria.
7.19: And they will come and all of them will rest in the desolate valleys and in holes of the rocks, and on all the thorns and on all the watering holes.
7.20: In that day the Lord will shave with a razor that is hired—with those beyond the River, with the king of Assyria—the head and the hair of the legs and also the beard He will sweep away.
7.21: And it will come to pass in that day, that a man will keep alive a young cow of the herd and two sheep.
7.22: And it will come to pass from the abundance of producing milk, that he will eat curds—for everyone remaining in the midst of the land will eat curds and honey.
7.23: And it will come to pass in that day that there will be in every place where there are a thousand vines at a thousand of silver—there will be briers and thorns.
7.24: He comes there with arrows and with the bow, for all the land will become briers and thorns.
7.25: And all the hills that are kept in order with the hoe there, you will no longer go for fear of brier and thorn; and it will become for the sending forth of cattle and for the treading of sheep.

8.1: And YHWH said to me, “Take for yourself a large scroll and write on it with a pen of man: Maher-shalal-hash-baz.
8.2: And I will appoint for Myself faithful witnesses—Uriah the priest and Zechariah son of Jeberekiah.”
8.3: And I came near to the prophetess, and she conceived and gave birth to a son. And YHWH said to me, “Call his name Maher-shalal-hash-baz—
8.4: for before the child knows to cry, ‘My father and My mother,’ one will lift away the wealth of Damascus and the plunder of Samaria before the face of the king of Assyria!”
8.5: And YHWH continued to speak to me again, saying,
8.6: “Because that this people has rejected the waters of Shiloah, those going gently, and are rejoicing with Rezin and the son of Remaliah,
8.7: therefore, behold! The Lord is bringing up over them the waters of the River mighty and great—the king of Assyria and all his glory—and it will go up over all its channels and it will go over all its banks,
8.8: and it will pass on into Judah, overflowing and passing over—reaching to the neck; and the stretching out of its wings and is the fullness of the breadth of your land, O Immanuel!
8.9: Join together, O peoples, and be shattered! And give ear, all you distant lands—Gird yourselves and be shattered, gird yourselves and be shattered!
8.10: Take counsel—counsel—and it will be broken; speak a word; it will not stand, for God is with us.
8.11: For thus YHWH spoke to me, with a strong hand, and He admonished me from walking in the way of this people, saying:
8.12: “You? shall not say conspiracy to everything that this people says is conspiracy; and its fear you? should not fear, and you should not dread.
8.13: YHWH of Hosts—Him You? shall sanctify, and He is your? Fear, and He is your? Dread.
8.14: And He be for a sanctuary, and for a stone of striking and for a rock of falling for the two houses of Israel, for a trap and for a snare for those dwelling in Jerusalem!
8.15: And many will stumble over them, and they will fall and be broken and be snared and captured.”
8.16: Bind up the testimony, and seal the law among my disciples.
8.17: And I will wait for YHWH, the One hiding His face from the house of Jacob, and I will hope in Him.
8.18: Here am I and the children whom YHWH has given to me as signs and as wonders in Israel, from YHWH of Hosts, the One dwelling on Mount Zion.
8.19: And when they say to you?, “Seek after the spirits and after the spiritists who whisper and mutter—” a people should not seek after its gods, on behalf of the living unto the dead,
8.20: to the law and to the testimony? If they do not speak this according to the word, it is because there is not in them dawn.
8.21: And he will pass over into it hard pressed and hungry, and it will be that when he is hungry and becomes angry, that he will think little of his king and of his gods, and he will turn upward,
8.22: and will look to the earth, and behold—distress and darkness, gloom of anguish, and he will be driven away into the dark.

9.1: But there is no gloom for the one who is in distress herself. In an earlier time He treated lightly the land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali, and afterward He will honor the Way of the Sea, beyond the Jordan, Galilee of the nations.
9.2: The people who are walking in the darkness have seen a great light; for those sitting in the land of the shadow of death, a light has shone upon them.
9.3: You have multiplied the nation. Have you not have increased the joy? They rejoice before Your face according to the joy of the harvest, like those who rejoice in dividing the plunder.
9.4: For the yoke of his burden, and the staff of his shoulder—the rod of one pressing on him—You have broken as on the day of Midian.
9.5: For every boot treading with quaking, and the garment rolled in blood, will be for burning, as fuel for fire.
9.6: For a child has been born unto us, a son has been given unto us, and the government will be upon his shoulder. And his name will be called Wonderful, Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace.
9.7: Of the increase of His government and peace will be no end, over the throne of David and over his kingdom, to establish it and sustain it with justice and with righteousness from now and even to forever. The zeal of YHWH of Hosts will accomplish this.
9.8: YHWH has sent a word against Jacob, and it has fallen upon Israel;
9.9: and the people will know all of it—Ephraim and the one dwelling in Samaria—in pride arrogance of heart they will say:
9.10: “The bricks have fallen down, and with hewn stone we will rebuild; the sycamores are cut down, and with cedars we will replace them .”
9.11: And YHWH raises up the adversaries of Rezin against him and his enemies He joins together,
9.12: Aram from the east and Philistia from the west, and they will devour Israel with a whole mouth. In all this, His anger is not turned back and still His hand is stretched out.
9.13: And the people did not turn to the One striking it, and YHWH of Hosts they did not seek.
9.14: And YHWH will cut off from Israel the head and tail, palm branch and reed, in a single day.
9.15: The elder and the uplifted of face—he is the head; and the prophet teaching lies—he is the tail.
9.16: The guides for this people mislead them, and those they mislead are being swallowed up.
9.17: Upon thus YHWH does not rejoice in its young men, and on its fatherless and widows He has no compassion, for everyone is godless and an evildoer, and every mouth speaks foolishness. In all this His anger is not turned away and still His hand is stretched out.
9.18: For wickedness burns as the fire, and it will devour brier and thorn; and it is kindled in the thickets of the forest, and they mount up in a rising of smoke.
9.19: By the wrath of YHWH of hosts the land is scorched, and the people are fuel for the fire; a man has no pity upon his brother.
9.20: And one cuts down on the right, and is hungry, and he eats on the left, and they have not been satisfied; each man devours the flesh of his own arm.
9.21: Manasseh Ephraim, and Ephraim Manasseh—together they are against Judah. In all this His anger has not turned back, and still His hand is stretched out.

10.1: Woe to those decreeing decrees of iniquity and writers writing perverseness,
10.2: to turn aside justness from the poor, and to tear away justice from the lowly of My people, that widows may be their prey, and fatherless their plunder!
10.3: And what will you? do in the day of reckoning, and at the desolation that comes from afar? Upon whom will you? flee for help, and where will you? leave your? wealth?
10.4: Nothing remains but to kneel down among the prisoners, and among the slain they will fall. In all this, His anger is not turned back and His hand is still stretched out.
10.5: Woe to Assyria, the rod of My anger, and the staff that is in their hand is My rage.
10.6: I will send him against a godless nation and I will order him against the people of My wrath, to plunder the plunder and to prey on the prey, and to set them for trampling like the clay of the streets.
10.7: And he does not consider it so, and his heart does not reckon it so, for to destroy is in his heart, and to cut off nations—not a few.
10.8: For he says: “Are not my princes all kings?
10.9: Or is not Calno like Carchemish? Or is not Hamath like Arpad? Is not Samaria like Damascus?
10.10: As my hand has reached to the kingdoms of the idols and their graven images above those of Jerusalem and Samaria—
10.11: is it not that as I have done to Samaria and her idols, thus I will do to Jerusalem and her idols?’”
10.12: And it will come to pass, when YHWH has cut off all his work against Mount Zion and against Jerusalem: “I will reckon with the fruit of the magnitude of the heart of the king of Assyria and with the splendor of the loftiness of his eyes.
10.13: For he says, ‘By the power of my hand I have done this , and by my own wisdom, for I have understanding; and I have removed the boundaries of the peoples, and their treasuries I have plundered; and I have brought down like a mighty one those sitting!
10.14: And my hand has found the riches of the peoples as a nest; and as a gathering of forsaken eggs I have gathered all the earth; and there has not been one flapping a wing or opening its mouth and chirping.’”
10.15: Will the ax glorify itself over the one hewing with it? Or will the saw exalt itself over one waving it—waving it as a rod of the one raising it, lifting it up as a staff not of wood!
10.16: Therefore the Lord YHWH of Hosts will send among his fat ones leanness, and in place of his glory He will kindle a burning like the burning of fire.
10.17: And the Light of Israel will be for a fire, and his Holy One for a flame, and it will burn and devour his thorn and his brier in one day.
10.18: And the glory of his forest and his orchard He will finish off from soul to flesh, and he will become like the melting away of one being sick.
10.19: And the remnant of the trees of his forest will be so few that a child could count them!
10.20: And it will come to be in that day that the remnant of Israel and the survivor of the house of Jacob, will not continue anymore to lean on the one striking it. And he will lean truly on YHWH, the Holy One of Israel.
10.21: A remnant will return, a remnant of Jacob, to the Mighty God.
10.22: For though your people O Israel, should be as the sand of the sea, only a remnant will return of it. The destruction is decreed, overflowing with righteousness.
10.23: For the full end is decreed, that the Lord YHWH of Hosts will make in the midst of all the land.
10.24: Therefore thus says the Lord YHWH of Hosts: “You should not fear, O My people, dwelling in Zion, the Assyrian! With a rod he will strike you, and his staff he will lift up against you in the way of Egypt.
10.25: For in just a very little while, My rage will come to an end, and My anger will be upon their destruction.”
10.26: And YHWH of Hosts will stir up against him a whip, like the striking of Midian at the rock of Oreb; and as His rod was over the sea, so will He lift it up in the manner of Egypt.
10.27: And in that day and it will come to pass that his burden will turned aside from your shoulder and his yoke from your neck and the yoke will be destroyed from the face of fatness,
10.28: he has come to Ayyah, he has passed over Migron; at Michmash he attends to his vessels.
10.29: They have passed over at the ford; Geba was a lodging-place for them . Ramah has trembled, Gibeah of Saul has fled.
10.30: Cry aloud with your voice, O daughter of Gallim! Pay attention, O Laish, O poor Anathoth!
10.31: Madmenah has fled, those dwelling in Gebim have taken refuge.
10.32: Yet today he will remain at Nob; he will wave his fist at the mountain of the daughter of Zion, the hill of Jerusalem.
10.33: Behold, the Lord YHWH of Hosts will lop off the bough with a crash; and those being high of stature will be cut down, and the high ones will be brought low.
10.34: And He will cut down the thickets of the forest with iron, and Lebanon will fall before the Mighty One.

11.1: A branch there will come forth from the stump of Jesse, and a fresh shoot from his roots will bear fruit.
11.2: And will rest upon Him the Spirit of YHWH, the Spirit of wisdom and understanding, the Spirit of counsel and might, the Spirit of knowledge and fear of YHWH.
11.3: And His delight is in the fear of YHWH, and by the sight of His eyes He will not judge, and not by the hearing of his ears He will decide.
11.4: And He will judge the lowly with righteousness, and He will decide with straightness for the poor of the earth; and He will strike the earth with the rod of His mouth, and with the breath of His lips He will slay the wicked.
11.5: And righteousness will be the girdle of His waist, and faithfulness the girdle of His loins.
11.6: And the wolf will dwell with the lamb, and the leopard will lie down with the young goat, and the calf and the young lion and the fatling together, and a little child will lead them.
11.7: And the cow and the bear will graze; their young will lie down together, and the lion like the ox will eat straw.
11.8: And the suckling will play over the hole of a cobra, and over the den of a viper the one being weaned will stretch out his hand.
11.9: They will not harm and they will not destroy in all the mountain of My holiness; for the earth will be full of the knowledge of YHWH, as the waters are covering the sea.
11.10: And in that day it will come to pass that the root of Jesse, which is standing, will be as a banner for the peoples; The nations will seek Him out, and His resting place will be in glory.
11.11: And it will come to pass in that day that the Lord will add a second time with His hand to recover the remnant of His people who will remain from Assyria, and from Egypt, and from Pathros, and from Cush, and from Elam, and from Shinar, and from Hamath, and from the islands of the sea.
11.12: And He will lift up a banner for the nations, and He will gather the banished ones of Israel, and the dispersed ones of Judah He will gather together from the four wings of the earth.
11.13: And the jealousy of Ephraim will be turned aside, and the adversaries of Judah will be cut off. Ephraim will not envy Judah and Judah will not harass Ephraim.
11.14: And they will fly down on the shoulder of the Philistines westward; together they will plunder the sons of the east. Upon Edom and Moab they will send forth their hands, and the sons of Ammon will be their subjects.
11.15: And YHWH will devote to destruction the tongue of the Sea of Egypt, and He will wave His hand over the River with the might of His wind, and will strike it into seven streams, for one to tread in sandals.
11.16: And there will be a highway for the remnant of His people who remain from Assyria, as it was for Israel in the day of his coming up from the land of Egypt.

12.1: And you will say in that day, “I will praise You, O YHWH! Though You were angry with me, Your nose has turned away, and You have comforted me.
12.2: Behold, God is my salvation; I will trust and not be afraid, for my strength and song is YAH—YHWH, and He has become for me salvation.”
12.3: And you? will draw water with joy from the springs of salvation.
12.4: And you? will say in that day: “Give praise to YHWH; call on His name! Declare among the peoples His deeds; cause it to be remembered that His Name is exalted.
12.5: Sing to YHWH, for He has done magnificent things ; let this be known in all the earth.
12.6: Cry out and ring out, O dweller of Zion, for great among you is the Holy One of Israel.”

13.1: The burden of Babylon that Isaiah son of Amoz has seen.
13.2: Upon the bare mountain lift up a banner! Raise high your voice to them. Wave a hand, that they may enter the gates of the nobles.
13.3: I have commanded My holy ones; also, I have called My mighty ones for My nose—those jubilant ones of My pride.
13.4: The voice of a crowd on the mountains, like that of many people—the voice of a roar of the kingdoms of nations gathered together! YHWH of Hosts is mustering a host for battle!
13.5: They are coming from a land far off, from the end of the heavens—YHWH and the instruments of His indignation—to destroy all the land.
13.6: Wail, for the day of YHWH is near; it will come as destruction from Shaddai.
13.7: At this all hands will go limp, and every heart of man will melt;
13.8: and they will be afraid; pains and pangs will seize them; like a woman in labor they will writhe. Each man to his neighbor will look with astonishment, with faces aflame—their faces.
13.9: Behold, the day of YHWH is coming, cruel with wrath and heat of anger, to set the land unto desolation, and He will destroy its sinful ones from it.
13.10: For the stars of the heavens and their constellations their light will not shine forth; the sun will be darkened in its going forth, and the moon will not cause to shine its light.
13.11: And I will visit punishment upon the world for evil, and upon the wicked for their iniquity, and I will make cease the arrogance of the proud ones, and the haughtiness of the ruthless ones I will lay low.
13.12: I will make man scarcer than pure gold, and mankind than the gold of Ophir.
13.13: Upon thus I will make the heavens tremble and the earth will be shaken from her place, by the fury of YHWH of Hosts, and in the day of the heat of his nose.
13.14: And it has been like the gazelle driven away, or like a sheep without one gathering it , each man to his people will turn, and each man to his land will flee.
13.15: Everyone who is found will be pierced, and everyone who is captured will fall by the sword;
13.16: and their children will be dashed to pieces before their eyes; their houses will be plundered and their wives will be ravished.
13.17: Behold, I am stirring up against them the Medes, who regard have no for silver, and gold—they do not delight in it.
13.18: And the bows will dash to pieces the young men, and on the fruit of the womb they will have no mercy—on sons their eye has no pity.
13.19: Babylon, the glory of kingdoms and the beauty of the pride of the Chaldeans, will be like the overthrowing by God of Sodom and Gomorrah.
13.20: She wil not be dwelt in to eternity, she wil not be dwelt in for generation and generation, and no Arab or shepherds will pitch a tent; there they will not make flocks rest there.
13.21: And desert creatures will stretch out there, and their houses will be full of howling creatures, and daughters of the ostrich will dwell there, and wild goats will skip about there,
13.22: and hyenas will howl in its towers, and jackals in its palaces of delight. And her time is coming near, and her days will not be drawn out.

14.1: For YHWH will have compassion on Jacob, and again He will choose Israel, and He will settle them in their own land, and the sojourner will join them with them, and attach themselves to the house of Jacob.
14.2: And the peoples will take them and bring them to their place, and the house of Israel will possess them in the land of YHWH as menservants and as maidservants, and they will become captives to their captors, and they will rule over their oppressors.
14.3: And it will come to pass in the day of YHWH giving rest to you from your pain and from your trouble and the hard servitude that has been served upon you,
14.4: that you will take up this proverb against the king of Babylon, and you will say: “How the oppressor has ceased! The boisterous rage has ceased!
14.5: YHWH has broken the staff of the wicked, the scepter of the rulers—
14.6: the one striking the peoples in wrath, a striking with no turning aside, one ruling the nations in anger, pursuing without restraint.
14.7: All the earth is at rest and quiet; they burst forth with singing,
14.8: even the cypress trees rejoice over you, the cedars of Lebanon: “Since you have been laid low, the cutter has not come up against us.”
14.9: Sheol from beneath stirs itself for you, to meet you at your coming; it rouses for you the Rephaim, all the chief ones of the earth; it raises up from their thrones all the kings of the nations.
14.10: All of them will answer and will say to you, “Even you have become weak like us, have you become like us.”
14.11: Your pride has been brought down to Sheol, the sound of your harps; under you is spread out the maggot, and your covering is the worm.
14.12: How you have fallen from the heavens, O shining one, son of the dawn! You have been cut down to the ground, O weakener of nations!
14.13: For you have said in your heart, “I will ascend to the heavens; above the stars of God I will raise my throne. And I will sit on the mountain of assembly, in the far reaches of the north.
14.14: I will ascend above the heights of the clouds; I will be like to the Most High.”
14.15: Yet you will be brought down to Sheol, to the sides of the Pit.
14.16: Those who see you will gaze at you, they will ponder at you: “ Is this the man who made tremble the earth, the one shaking kingdoms,
14.17: setting the world as a wilderness; its cities he has broken down; his captives he has not released to their house?”
14.18: All the kings of the nations, all of them, lie down in glory, each man in his own house;
14.19: and you are cast out from your grave like a detested branch, the garment of those who are slain, pierced by the sword, going down to the stones of the pit, like a corpse trampled.
14.20: You will not be joined with them in the grave, because your land you have destroyed, your people you have killed—the seed of evildoers let not be mentioned forever!
14.21: Prepare slaughter for his sons, for the iniquity of their fathers—lest they rise up and possess the land and fill the face of the world with cities!
14.22: “And I will rise up against them,” a declaration of YHWH of Hosts, “And I will cut off from Babylon the name, and remnant, and offspring, and posterity,” a declaration of YHWH.
14.23: “Into a possession into a possession for the owl, and ponds of waters, and I will sweep it with the broom of destruction,” a declaration of YHWH of Hosts.
14.24: YHWH of Hosts has sworn, saying, “Surely as I have purposed, so it will be, and as I have counseled, so it will stand:
14.25: To break Assyria in My land, and on My mountains I will trample him! And his yoke will be turned aside from them and his burden will be turned aside from their shoulders.”
14.26: This is the counsel that is counseled for all the earth, and this is the hand that is stretched out over all the nations.
14.27: For YHWH of Hosts has counseled, and who can break it ? And His hand is stretched out, and who can turn it back?
14.28: In the year of the death of King Ahaz came this burden:
14.29: Not do rejoice, Philistia, all of you, that the rod that struck you is broken—for from the root of the snake will come forth a viper, and its fruit is a flying fiery serpent!
14.30: And the firstborn of the poor will find pasture, and the needy will stretch out in safety; and I will kill your root with famine and it will slay your remnant.
14.31: Wail, O gate! Cry out, O city! Melt away, Philistia, all of you—for smoke will come from the north, and none is alone in its ranks.
14.32: And what will he answer to the messengers of the nation? That YHWH has founded Zion, and in it the poor of His people will take refuge.

15.1: The burden of Moab: Because in a night Ar of Moab is laid waste, it has been cut off! Because in a night Kir of Moab is laid waste, it has been cut off!
15.2: He has gone up to Bajith and to Dibon, to the high places to weep. Over Nebo and over Medeba Moab is wailing. On all its heads is baldness, and every beard is cut off.
15.3: In its streets they have girded on sackcloth; on its rooftops and in the square, everyone wails, going down with weeping.
15.4: And Heshbon and Elealeh cry out; as far as Jahaz their voice is heard. Upon thus the armed ones of Moab cry out; his soul trembles within him.
15.5: My heart cries out for Moab, for her fugitives are as far as Zoar, to Eglath-Shelishiyyah; for the Ascent of Luhith—with weeping he goes up on it; for on the road of Horonaim they raise up a cry from destruction.
15.6: For the waters of Nimrim have become desolations, for the grass has withered away; the vegetation is finished—nothing green is there.
15.7: Upon thus, the abundance he made and their store, they carry them over the Brook of the Willows.
15.8: For the outcry has gone all around the borders of Moab; as far as Eglaim is her howling, and her howling is in Beer-elim.
15.9: For the waters of Dimon have been full of blood, for I will put upon Dimon added things : A lion for the survivor of Moab, and for the remnant of the land.

16.1: Send the lamb to the ruler of the land, from Sela in the wilderness, to the mountain of the daughter of Zion.
16.2: And it will come to pass that , like a fluttering bird or a nestling cast out, so shall be the daughters of Moab at the fords of the Arnon.
16.3: “Bring in counsel; make judgment; set like the night your shadow in the middle of noontime. Hide those driven away, do not uncover the one fleeing.
16.4: Let sojourn among you the outcasts of Moab; be a shelter for them from the face of the destroyer.” For the extortioner comes to an end and destruction ceases; the tramplers of the land are finished off.
16.5: And in loving devotion a throne will be established, and one will sit on it in faithfulness in the tent of David—one judging and seeking justice, and hastening righteousness.
16.6: We have heard of the pride of Moab—exceeding pride—of his arrogance and his pride and his wrath; not so are his lies.
16.7: Therefore Moab will wail; let everyone wail for Moab; for the raisin-cakes of Kir-Hareseth you? will mourn, utterly stricken.
16.8: For the fields of Heshbon languish, and the vine of Sibmah. The lords of the nations have struck down its choicest vines, which had reached as far as Jazer, and wandered to the desert—her branches stretched out and passed over the sea.
16.9: Upon thus I weep with the weeping of Jazer, for the vine of Sibmah, I will drench you with my tears, O Heshbon and Elealeh. For over your summer fruit—over your summer fruit the shouting have fallen silent.
16.10: And gladness and rejoicing are gathered up from the field of planting, and in the vineyards there is no singing, and no shouting, and the wine in the presses, the treader does not tread; their shouting I have made cease.
16.11: Upon thus my bowels do moan for Moab like a harp, and my inmost being for Kir-heres.
16.12: And it will come to pass, when it is seen that Moab has become weary on the high place, that he will come to his sanctuary to pray; he will not prevail.
16.13: This is the word that YHWH has spoken to Moab from that time;
16.14: and YHWH has spoken, saying, “In three years, according to the years of a hireling, then the glory of Moab will be turned to dishonor, with all the great multitude; and the remnant is little—small, not mighty.”

17.1: The burden of Damascus: “Behold, Damascus is being turned aside as a city, and it has become a heap of ruins.
17.2: The cities of Aroer are forsaken; they will be for flocks to lie down, and no one will frighten them.
17.3: The fortress will cease from Ephraim, and the kingdom from Damascus; and the remnant of Aram will become like the glory of the sons of Israel,” a declaration of YHWH of hosts.
17.4: “And it will come to pass in that day, that the glory of Jacob will hang low, and the fatness of his flesh will become lean;
17.5: and it will be as the gathering of the harvester of the standing grain, when his arm harvests the ears; and it will be as the gathering of the ears in the Valley of Rephaim;
17.6: and there will be left over in it gleanings, like the shaking of an olive tree, two or three berries at the top of the bough, four or five bearing fruit in its branches.” A declaration of YHWH God of Israel.
17.7: In that day man will gaze upon his Maker, and his eyes will look unto the Holy One of Israel.
17.8: And he will not look to the altars, the work of his hands, and that which his fingers have made he will not see—the Asherahs and incense altars.
17.9: In that day the cities of his strength will be like the abandoned forest and summit that they abandoned before the faces of the sons of Israel; and it will be a desolation.
17.10: For you have forgotten the God of your salvation, and the Rock of your refuge you have not remembered. Upon thus you may plant plants of pleasantness, and you may sow vines of a stranger,
17.11: on the day you plant you make it grow, and in the morning of your sowing, you make it sprout; the harvest will flee on the day of the coming of sickness and pain that cannot be cured.
17.12: Woe to the multitude of many peoples—like the roaring of seas they roar! And the rumble of nations—like the rumbling of mighty waters they are crashing!
17.13: The peoples—like the rumbling of many waters they are crashing, and He will rebuke them, and they will flee far away, and they will be chased like chaff on the mountains before the face of the wind, and like a wheel of dust before the face of the whirlwind.
17.14: And at the time of evening, behold the terror; before the morning he is no more! This is the portion of those plundering us and the lot of those preying on us.

18.1: Woe to the land of whirring wings, which is beyond the rivers of Cush,
18.2: that is sending envoys by sea, and in vessels of papyrus over the face of the waters! Go, swift messengers, to a nation tall and smooth, to a people feared from that and onward, a nation of strength and trampling, whose land the rivers divide.
18.3: All you dwellers of the world and dwellers of the earth—at the lifting of a banner in the mountains, you? will see it , and at the blowing of a shofar, you? will hear it .
18.4: For thus has said YHWH to me: “I will take My rest, and I will look on from My settled place, shimmering like heat upon the light, like a cloud of dew in the heat of harvest.”
18.5: For before the face of the harvest of the bud at the filling and the blossom is a ripening grape, then He will cut off the tendrils with pruning hooks, and the branches He will turn aside and strike off.
18.6: They will be left behind together for the birds of prey of the mountains and for the beasts of the earth; and the bird of prey will feed in summer on them, and all the beasts of the earth will feed in winter on them.
18.7: At that time a gift will be brought to YHWH of Hosts from a people tall and smooth, and from a people feared from afar, a nation of strength and treading down, for whom its land the rivers divides; to the place of the name of YHWH of Hosts, to Mount Zion.

19.1: The burden of Egypt: Behold, YHWH is riding on a swift cloud, and He is coming into Egypt; and the idols of Egypt will quiver before His face, and the heart of Egypt will melt within them.
19.2: “And I will set Egyptian against Egyptian, and a man will be fighting against his brother, and a man against his neighbor—city against city, kingdom against kingdom.
19.3: And the spirit of Egypt will be emptied out from among them, and I will swallow up its counsel; and they will resort to the idols, and to the charmers, and to the mediums, and to the spiritists.
19.4: And I will shut up Egypt in the hand of harsh masters, and a fierce king will rule over them,” a declaration of the Lord YHWH of hosts.
19.5: And the waters of the Sea will dry up, and the River will become parched and dried up;
19.6: and the rivers will trickle and will become foul, and the brooks of Egypt dried out; the reeds and rushes will wither,
19.7: the rushes by the River, upon the mouth of the River, and every sown field of the River will wither; it will be driven away and be no more.
19.8: And the fishermen will mourn, and all those casting a hook into the River will lament, and those spreading nets upon the face of the waters, they will languish.
19.9: And those working fine flax will be put to shame, and those weaving white cloth,
19.10: and her weavers have been crushed, and all the hired workers are stagnant of soul.
19.11: Indeed the princes of Zoan are fools, the counsel of the wise counselors of Pharaoh has become dull. How can you? say to Pharaoh, “A son of wise men, I am a son of kings from times before?
19.12: Where then are your wise ones? And let them declare it to you now and make known what YHWH of Hosts has counseled for Egypt!
19.13: The princes of Zoan have become fools, the princes of Memphis are deceived; they have led Egypt astray—the cornerstone of her tribes.
19.14: YHWH has mixed into her midst a spirit of distortions, and Egypt they have led astray in all her work, like the staggering of a drunkard in his vomit.
19.15: And there will not be work for Egypt that one could do—head or tail, palm branch or reed,
19.16: In that day Egypt will become like women, and it will tremble and feel dread from the face of the waving of the hand of YHWH of Hosts, which He is waving over it.
19.17: And the land of Judah will be to Egypt as a terror; everyone who makes mention of it will tremble in himself from the face of the counsel of YHWH of Hosts, which He is counseling against Egypt .
19.18: In that day there will be five cities in the land of Egypt speaking the language of Canaan and swearing oaths by YHWH of Hosts. “City of the Sun” it will be said of one.
19.19: In that day there will be an altar to YHWH in the midst of the land of Egypt, and a pillar to YHWH at its border.
19.20: And it will be as a sign and as a witness to YHWH of hosts in the land of Egypt; for they will cry to YHWH from the face of the oppressors, and He will send to them one saving and defending, and He will deliver them.
19.21: And YHWH will make Himself known to Egypt, and Egypt will acknowledge YHWH on that day, and they will prepare sacrifice and offering, and they will vow a vow to YHWH and fulfill it .
19.22: And YHWH will strike Egypt, striking and healing; and they will turn back to YHWH, and He will be entreated by them, and He will heal them.
19.23: That in the day there will be a highway from Egypt to Assyria, and Assyria will come into Egypt, and Egypt into Assyria, and Egypt with Assyria will serve Him .
19.24: In that day Israel will be the third with Egypt and with Assyria, a blessing in the midst of the earth,
19.25: when YHWH of Hosts will bless him, saying, “Blessed be My people Egypt, and the work of My hands Assyria, and My inheritance Israel.”

20.1: In the year of the coming of Tartan to Ashdod, when Sargon the king of Assyria sent him, then he fought against Ashdod and captured it.
20.2: At the time that YHWH spoke by the hand of Isaiah son of Amoz, saying, “Go and remove the sackcloth from your body, and draw off your sandals from your feet.” And he did so, walking naked and barefoot.
20.3: And YHWH said, “As My servant Isaiah has walked naked and barefoot three years as a sign and a wonder against Egypt and against Cush,
20.4: so the king of Assyria will lead away the captive of Egypt and the exile of Cush—young and old—naked and barefoot, and their buttocks uncovered, the nakedness of Egypt.
20.5: And they will be dismayed and ashamed of Cush as their hope and Egypt as their glory.
20.6: And the one dwelling on this seacoast will say in that day, ‘Behold, this was our hope, where we would flee there for help to be delivered from the king of Assyria. How then can we escape?’”

21.1: The burden of the wilderness the Sea: Like whirlwinds in the Negev passing through, it comes from the wilderness, from a fearsome land.
21.2: A harsh vision is declared to me: “The treacherous one deals treacherously, and the destroyer destroys. Go up, O Elam! Besiege, O Media! All her sighing I have made cease.”
21.3: Upon thus my loins are filled with pain; pangs have taken hold of me like the pangs of one giving birth. I am writhing from hearing; I am disturbed from seeing.
21.4: My heart staggers, a shuddering terrifies me; the twilight of my desire has been set for me into fear,
21.5: to arrange the table and lay out the spread, to eat, to drink. Rise up, O princes, and anoint the shield!
21.6: For thus the Lord has said to me: “Go, station the one keeping watch and let him declare what he sees.
21.7: And he sees a chariot with a pair of horses, the rider of a donkey, and the rider of a camel, and he will be attentive, with great attention.”
21.8: And the lookout called out: “Upon the watchtower, my Lord, I stand continually by day, and at my post I take my stand every night.
21.9: And behold this, the chariot of a man is coming, with a pair of horses.” And he answered and said, “Fallen, Babylon has fallen! And all the images of her gods He has broken to the ground!”
21.10: O my threshing, and the son of my threshing floor, that which I have heard from YHWH of Hosts, the God of Israel, I have declared to you?!
21.11: The burden of Dumah: To me one is calling from Seir: “Watchman, what of the night? Watchman, what of the night?”
21.12: The watchman said, “The morning comes, and also the night. If you? would inquire, then inquire; return, come.”
21.13: The burden upon Arabia: In the thicket you? lodge in Arabia, O caravans of Dedanites,
21.14: to meet the thirsty one. Bring water, O dwellers of the land of Tema, with his bread meet the one fleeing.
21.15: For they do flee from the face of swords, from the sword drawn, and from the bow bent, and from the weight of war.
21.16: For thus says the Lord to me: “Within a year, according to the years of a hireling, then will end all the glory of Kedar.
21.17: And the remnant of the number of archers, the mighty ones of the sons of Kedar, will be few.” For YHWH, the God of Israel, has spoken.

22.1: The burden of the Valley of Vision: What with you then, that you have gone up, all of you, to the rooftops,
22.2: full of noise O city murmuring, O jubilant town? Your slain are not those slain by the sword, and they were not killed in battle.
22.3: All your rulers have fled together, without a bow they are bound; all those being found in you are bound together; they have fled far away.
22.4: Upon thus I said, “Look away from me, let me be bitter; do not hurry in my weeping to comfort me over the destruction of the daughter of my people!”
22.5: For it is a day of tumult and trampling and confusion unto the Lord YHWH of Hosts in the Valley of Vision, digging down a wall and crying to the mountain.
22.6: And Elam bore the quiver with chariots of man and horsemen, and Kir uncovered the shield.
22.7: And it will come to pass that that the choicest of your valleys will be full of of chariots, and the horsemen are set diligently at the gate.
22.8: And He uncovered the screen of Judah. And You looked on that day to the weapons of the House of the Forest.
22.9: And the breaches of the city of David you? saw, that they were many. And you? gathered together the waters of the lower pool.
22.10: And the houses of Jerusalem you? numbered, and you? broke down the houses to fortify the wall.
22.11: And you? made a reservoir between the two walls for the water of the old pool, and you? did not look to the one who made it, and Him who fashioned it long ago you? did not see.
22.12: And the Lord YHWH of Hosts called on that day for weeping, and for wailing, and for baldness, and for girding with sackcloth.
22.13: And behold, joy and gladness, slaying of oxen and slaughtering of sheep, the eating of flesh and drinking of wine: “Let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we die!”
22.14: And it was revealed in my ears by YHWH of Hosts: “This iniquity will not be atoned for from you? even until you? die,” says the Lord YHWH of Hosts.
22.15: Thus says the Lord YHWH of Hosts: “Go, come to this steward, to Shebna who is over the house:
22.16: What is unto you here, and who is unto you here, that have hewn out for yourself here a tomb, hewing out his tomb on high, carving a resting place in the rock for himself?
22.17: Behold, YHWH is going to hurl you with a great hurling, and will grasp hold of you with a firm grasp, O strong one;
22.18: He will wind you round and round, tossing you like a ball into a land broad of sides; you will die there, and there the chariots of your glory will be the shame of the house of your master!
22.19: And I will thrust you from your station, and one will throw you down from your position.
22.20: And it will be on that day, I will call My servant Eliakim son of Hilkiah,
22.21: and I will clothe him with your tunic and strengthen him with your sash, and I will give your rule into his hand; and He will be as a father to those dwelling in Jerusalem and to the house of Judah.
22.22: And I will place the key of the house of David upon his shoulder. And he will open, and no one is shutting; and he will shut, and no one is opening.
22.23: And I will fasten him like a peg in a place that is firm, and he will become as a throne of glory to the house of his father.
22.24: And they will hang on him all the glory of the house of his father—the offspring and the posterity, and all the vessels of small size , from all the cups, and unto all the vessels of jars.
22.25: In that day, a declaration of YHWH of Hosts, the peg that is fastened will give way, into a firm place, it will be sheared off, and fall, and the burden that was on it will be cut off,” for YHWH has spoken.

23.1: The Burden of Tyre: Wail, O ships of Tarshish, for it is laid waste—without house, without harbor! From the land of Kittim it has been revealed to them.
23.2: Be still, O dwellers of the coastland, merchants of Sidon, whose traders have crossed the sea.
23.3: And on great waters is the seed of Sihor; the harvest of the River is her revenue, and she is a marketplace of nations.
23.4: Be ashamed, O Sidon, for the sea has spoken, the strength of the sea, saying: “I have not been pained and I have not brought forth children, and I have not nourished young men or brought up young women.”
23.5: When the report comes to Egypt they will writhe in agony at the report of Tyre.
23.6: Pass over to Tarshish; Wail, O dwellers of the coastland.
23.7: Is this your? jubilant one, whose origin is from days of old? Her own feet carried her to settle far away.
23.8: Who has taken counsel this against Tyre, the crowning one, whose merchants are princes; her traders are the honorable ones of the earth?
23.9: Yhwh of hosts has purposed it—to dishonor the pride of all glory, to shame all the honorable ones of the earth.
23.10: Cultivate your land like the Nile, O daughter of Tarshish; there is no girdle anymore.
23.11: He has stretched out His hand over the sea, He has shaken the kingdoms. Yhwh has commanded against Canaan to destroy her strongholds.
23.12: And He said, “You will not add again to exult, O oppressed virgin daughter of Sidon. To Kittim arise, pass over—even there is no rest unto you.”
23.13: Behold the land of the Chaldeans—this people was not. Assyria has founded it for the desert creatures; they set up its towers, they raised up its palaces—He has appointed her to ruin.
23.14: Wail, O ships of Tarshish, for your? refuge has been destroyed!
23.15: And it will come to pass in that day, that Tyre will be forgotten seventy years—as the days of one king. At the end of seventy years, it will come to pass to Tyre as the song of the harlot:
23.16: “Take a harp, go about the city, O forgotten harlot; make sweet melody, sing many a song, so that you may be remembered.”
23.17: And it will come to pass at the end of seventy years, that YHWH will visit Tyre, and she will return to her hire and commit fornication with all the kingdoms of the world on the face of the earth.
23.18: And her gain and her pay will be set apart for YHWH; it will not be stored up and not laid up, for unto those sitting before YHWH will be her gain, to eat to satisfaction, and for a surpassing covering.

24.1: Behold, YHWH is emptying the earth, and is making it waste; and He has twisted its surface and has scattered its dwellers.
24.2: And it will be—as with the people so with the priest, as with the servant so with his master, as with the maidservant so with her mistress, as with the buyer so with the seller, as with the lender so with the borrower, as with the creditor so with the lender of it.
24.3: The earth will be utterly emptied and utterly plundered, for YHWH has spoken this word.
24.4: The earth has mourned and has faded away; the world has languished and has faded away; the haughty have languished people of the earth.
24.5: And the earth is defiled under its dwellers, because they have transgressed the laws—they have passed ovr the decree, they have broken the everlasting covenant.
24.6: Upon thus a curse has consumed the earth, and those who dwell upon it are found guilty; thus those dwelling in the earth have been burned, and few men have been left.
24.7: The new wine has failed, the vine has languished; all the joyful of heart have groaned.
24.8: The joy of the tambourines has ceased; the noise of the jubilant ones has ended; the joy of the harp has ceased.
24.9: They will not drink wine with a song; strong drink is bitter to those drinking it.
24.10: The city of confusion has been broken down; every house is closed up from entering.
24.11: A cry over the wine is in the streets; all joy is darkened; the rejoicing of the land has been removed.
24.12: In the city a desolation has been left, and the gate is broken into a ruin.
24.13: For thus it shall be within the land in the midst of the nations, like the shaking of an olive tree, like the gleaning of grapes when the vintage is done.
24.14: They will lift up their voice; they will sing of the majesty of YHWH—they have cried aloud from the sea.
24.15: Upon thus, glorify YHWH in the dawning light, the name of YHWH God of Israel in the islands of the sea.
24.16: From the ends of the earth we have heard songs: “Glory to the Righteous One.” And I said, “Leanness is unto me! Leanness is unto me! Woe to me!” Treacherous dealers have dealt treacherously, and with treachery treacherous dealers have dealt treacherously.
24.17: Terror and pit and snare are upon you, O dweller of the earth.
24.18: And it will come to pass that he who flees from the sound of terror will fall into the pit, and he who comes up from the midst of the pit will be caught in the snare. For the windows on high have been opened, and the foundations of the earth are shaken.
24.19: The earth has been utterly broken apart; the earth has been utterly split open; the earth has been shaken violently.
24.20: The earth like a drunkard will stagger greatly, and it will totter like a hut; and transgression will be heavy upon it, and it will fall and never again rise.
24.21: And it will come to pass in that day, that YHWH will lay a charge upon the host of the high place in the high place and upon the kings of the earth on the earth.
24.22: And they will be gathered together—a gathering of bound ones in the pit, and they will be shut up in the prison, and after a multitude of days they will be punished.
24.23: The moon and will be confounded and the sun will be ashamed, for YHWH of hosts will reign on Mount Zion and in Jerusalem, and before His elders is glory.

25.1: O YHWH, You are my God! I will exalt You. I will praise Your name, for You have done a wonderful thing, counsel of old, in steadfast faithfulness.
25.2: For You have made the city a heap, the fortified city a ruin, a fortress of strangers from a city—forever it will never be rebuilt!
25.3: Upon thus a strong people will honor You; a city of ruthless nations will fear you.
25.4: For You have been a stronghold for the poor, a stronghold for the needy one in his distress, a refuge from the storm, a shade from the heat—for the breath of the ruthless ones is like a storm against a wall,
25.5: like heat in a dry land, the roar of strangers You humble—the heat in the shadow of a cloud, the song of the ruthless ones will be brought low.
25.6: And YHWH of Hosts will make on this on the mountain for all the peoples a feast of fat things, a feast of aged wines—fat things full of marrow, aged wines well-refined.
25.7: And He will swallow up on this mountain the face of the wrapping covering over all the peoples, and the veil woven over all the nations.
25.8: He will swallow up death forever, and the Lord YHWH will wipe away the tear from all faces, and He will turn away the scorn of His people from all the earth. For YHWH has spoken.
25.9: And one will say on that day, “Behold, this is our God; we have waited for Him and He has saved us. This is YHWH; we have waited for Him—let us rejoice and be glad in His salvation!”
25.10: For the hand of YHWH will rest on this mountain, and Moab will be trampled down under Him as is trampled down straw in the waters of dung.
25.11: And He will spread out His hands within it, as the swimmer spreads out to swim, and He will bring low its pride with the skill of his hands.
25.12: And the fortress of the high place of your walls He will bend down—He will bring it low, touching it down to the ground, into dust!

26.1: In that day, will be sung this song in the land of Judah: We have a strong city; He sets salvation for walls and a rampart.
26.2: Open the gates, that a righteous nation may enter in—one keeping faith.
26.3: You will keep the one steadfast of mind in perfect peace, because he trusts in You.
26.4: Trust in YHWH even to forever, for in YAH—YHWH— is a Rock of Ages.
26.5: For He has brought down those dwelling in a high place; the city up high He brings low—He brings it down to the ground, He makes it touch even to the dust.
26.6: The foot will trample it—the feet of the poor, the steps of the lowly.
26.7: The path of a righteous one is uprightness; You make level a straight track for the righteous one.
26.8: Indeed, in the path of Your judgments, O YHWH, we wait for You; for Your name and for the remembrance of You is the desire of the soul.
26.9: With my soul I have desired You in the night; yes, with my spirit within me I seek You early; for when Your judgments are on the earth, those dwelling in the world will learn righteousness.
26.10: Let the wicked be favored—he will not learn righteousness; in the land of uprightness he acts unjustly, and he cannot see the majesty of YHWH.
26.11: O YHWH, Your hand is lifted high; they do not see it . Let them see the zeal for the people and be ashamed; yes, the fire of Your enemies will devour them.
26.12: O YHWH, You will establish peace for us, even for all our works You have done for us.
26.13: O YHWH our God, lords besides You have ruled over us; only by You we make mention of Your name.
26.14: Those being dead will not live; the Rephaim will not rise; therefore you have attended to and destroyed them, and You have made to perish all memory of them.
26.15: You have added to the nation, O YHWH; You have added to the nation, You are glorified; You have expanded all the borders of the land.
26.16: O YHWH, in the distress they visited You; they poured out a whispering with Your discipline upon them.
26.17: Like a pregnant woman drawing near to giving birth writhes and cries out in pain, so have we been before Your face, O YHWH.
26.18: We were pregnant; we writhed; we gave birth as if to the wind; we have not accomplished salvation on earth, and those dwelling in the world have not fallen.
26.19: Your dead will live—my corpse they rise! Awake and sing out, O dwellers of the dust! For a dew of light is your dew, and the earth, she will cast out the Rephaim.
26.20: Go, my people, come into your chambers, and shut your doors behind you; hide yourself a little while, until His anger has passed by.
26.21: For behold, YHWH is coming forth from His place to punish the iniquity of those dwelling in the earth! And the earth will uncover her bloodshed and she will not cover anymore over her slain.

27.1: In that day YHWH will visit with His sword, severe and great and mighty, upon Leviathan the fleeing serpent, and upon Leviathan the coiling serpent, and He will kill the dragon that is in the sea.
27.2: In that day: Sing of her—a vineyard of delight!
27.3: I, YHWH, am watching over her; every moment I water her, lest one should visit upon her; night and day I watch over her.
27.4: Anger is not in Me. Who would set against Me a thorn—a thornbush? In the battle I will march against her, I will burn her alltogether.
27.5: Or if he should take hold of My strength, he will make peace with Me—peace he will make with Me.”
27.6: Those coming in of Jacob he causes to take root. Israel will blossom and bud, and they will fill the face of the world with fruit.
27.7: Has He has struck him as the striking of one striking him? As if according to the slaughter of his slain was he slayed?
27.8: In driving her off, in sending her out, You have contended with her; He removed it by His harsh wind, as on the day of the east wind.
27.9: Therefore by this the iniquity of Jacob will be wiped away, and this is all the fruit, to take away his sin: In His setting all the stones of the altar, as stones of chalk crushed to pieces; they will not rise—the Asherah poles and incense altars.
27.10: For the fortified city is alone, a habitation deserted and forsaken like the wilderness; there the calf will graze, and there he will lie down and consume her branches.
27.11: With the withering of her boughs, they are broken off; the women come make fires with it, for it is not for a people of understanding. Thus He who made them will not have compassion on them, and He who formed them will not show them favor.
27.12: And it will come to pass in that day, that YHWH will beat out the grain , from the stream of the River to the Wadi of Egypt, and you? will be gathered one by one, O sons of Israel.
27.13: And it will come to pass in that day that a great shofar will be blown, and will come those lost in the land of Assyria, and those driven away in the land of Egypt, and they will worship YHWH on the holy mountain in Jerusalem.

28.1: Woe, to the crown of pride, to the drunkards of Ephraim, and the withering flower, the beauty of his glory, which is at the head of the valley of fatness, those having been struck down by wine!
28.2: Behold, a mighty and strong one is unto the Lord, like a storm of hail and a tempest of destruction, like a flood of mighty waters overflowing, He will bring it to the ground with the hand.
28.3: With his feet, the pride of the crown of the drunkards of Ephraim will be trampled.
28.4: The fading flower, the beauty of his glory that is on the head of the valley of fatness, will be like a ripe fig before summer, which the one seeing it sees, and with it still in his palm, he swallows it.
28.5: In that day YHWH of Hosts will be a crown of glory and a diadem of beauty for the remnant of His people,
28.6: and as a spirit of justice for the one sitting in judgment, and as strength for those turning back the battle at the gate.
28.7: And also these through wine have erred, and with strong drink have wandered; priest and prophet have erred through strong drink and are swallowed up by the wine, they go astray through strong drink; they err in vision, they have stumbled in judgment.
28.8: For all the tables are full of vomit and filth without a place to stand.
28.9: To whom will He teach knowledge? And to whom will he explain the message? Those weaned from milk? Those removed from the breasts?
28.10: For, “Order on order, order on order; line on line, line on line; a little here, a little there.”
28.11: For with mocking lips and a foreign tongue He will speak to this people,
28.12: to whom He said: “This is the resting, may they give rest to the weary, and this is the refreshing,” they were not willing to listen.
28.13: And the word of YHWH will become to them “Order on order, order on order; line on line, line on line; a little here, a little there,” that they might go and stumble backward, and be broken and ensnared and captured.
28.14: Therefore hear the word of YHWH, O men of scorning, rulers of this people who are in Jerusalem.
28.15: For you? have said, “We have cut a covenant with death, and with Sheol we have made an agreement. The overflowing scourge, when it passes over, it will not come to us, for we have made falsehood our refuge, and in deception we have hidden ourselves.”
28.16: Therefore thus says the Lord YHWH: “Behold, I lay for a foundation in Zion a stone, a stone tested, a precious cornerstone, a sure foundation. One believing will not be in haste.
28.17: And I will make justice the measuring line, and righteousness the plumbline; and the hail will sweep away the refuge of lies, and the waters will overflow the hiding place.
28.18: And your? covenant with death will be dissolved, and your? agreement with Sheol will not stand; the overwhelming scourge, when it passes over, then you? will be trampled by it.
28.19: As often as it passes over, it will take you?; for morning by morning it will pass over.” And by day and by night, then it will be only a terror to understand the report.
28.20: For the bed is too short for stretching out, and the covering too narrow for wrapping oneself.
28.21: For as at Mount Perazim, YHWH will rise up; as in the Valley of Gibeon, He will rouse Himself, to do His work—His strange work! And to perform His task—His unusual task!
28.22: And now, do not mock, lest your? bonds be made strong; for destruction is decreed—I have heard from the Lord YHWH of Hosts—upon the whole land.
28.23: Give ear and hear my voice; pay attention and hear my speech.
28.24: Does the one plowing plow all the day to sow? He opens and harrows his ground.
28.25: Does he not, when he has leveled its face, then scatter caraway and sow cummin and put in a row the wheat in the appointed place and the barley and the spelt at its border?
28.26: And He instructs him in judgment, and His God teaches him.
28.27: For caraway is not threshed with a sledge, and the wheel of a cart is not rolled over the cumin. But with the staff caraway is beaten out, and the cummin with the rod.
28.28: Bread must be ground, but not forever does the one threshing thresh it; and he runs it over with the wheels of his cart, and his horses do not crush it.
28.29: Also this has gone forth from YHWH of Hosts, wonderful in counsel, great in wisdom.

29.1: Woe to Ariel, Ariel the city where David encamped! Add year to year; let the feasts come around.
29.2: And I will constrain Ariel, and there will be mourning and lamentation; and to Me she will be as Ariel!
29.3: And I will encamp in a circle against you, and I will lay siege against you with a siege-work, and I will raise against you the fortifications.
29.4: And you will be brought low, from the ground you will speak, and from the dust Your speech will be low, and Your voice will be from the ground like a spirit from the dust, and your speech will whisper.
29.5: The multitude of your foes will be like fine dust, and the multitude of the ruthless ones like chaff passing away; and it will come to pass in an instant, suddenly,
29.6: from YHWH of Hosts you will be visited with thunder and earthquake and a great voice, with storm and tempest and the flame of devouring fire.
